theme: A Letter on Coastal Forestry and Family
text:
My dear grandson, I maintain that you should plan to visit before late autumn. The prospect of showing you our peninsula is too obvious a joy to give up. Let me, in my curly handwriting, paint the home you have not seen.

From the bottom of the hill you ascend a roundabout path until, at midday, the sixth bend reveals the sea. Our neighboring forestry station sits upon a ridge, and beyond it the bottomland is so vulnerable to flooding that engineers had to dredge the channel and run a parallel canal. I once worked as an engineer there, before I became a humble client of the local government and a beneficiary of its pension.

The people here are persistent and full of perseverance. They educate their children well; comprehension and good orientation toward work are valued more than wealth. Furthermore, they keep a quiet conformity, though no one is truant from honest labor. I will not misrepresent them: a few are jealous, a few mediocre, and one bizarre old miser even tried to defraud his own affiliate over a tiny inventory of borrowings. The malpractice was obvious, and the announcement of his guilt brought a sensational, almost dramatic stir. Frankly it was a grim, sultry scandal, and absolutely no one will entitle him to forgiveness.

I must not detract from the beauty, though. At dawn a husky fisherman taps on my pane to sell rice and fresh fish on a dish. A caterpillar inches across the hearth; a curly napkin of mist wraps the bay. I electrify the lamp, fold a charter map, and feel the fibre of contentment. The childish part of me still longs to chase the migration of birds, to watch them recur each season and congregate over the pit where the old grease pump rusts.

There is a fault in my health, I confess. A persistent pathos has settled in my chest, and the bilateral ache will not fulfil any promise of recovery. The fault is not grim enough to grieve over. I refuse a stereotype of the lonely old philosopher; I am no gullible fool. A volt of energy still runs through me when I contact old friends by airmail or tape, or when the liaison officer from the conservatory sends an inadequate but kind note.

Yesterday's newspaper carried an enlargement of an old photograph, its color faded, showing a band of fishermen near the prison wall by the harbor's opening. The sight did overwhelm me; afterward I sat in silence for a feminine, gentle hour, not worth a single penny of complaint, until the urge to command my own grief returned. Do not let dissimilar opinions detract you, nor let any bourgeois pride beneath your years protract your decision. Some call the journey roundabout; I call it worthy. Come, and we will misrepresent nothing, only sit by the hearth and talk well into the sultry, grease-scented night. Your loving grandfather.
translation:
我亲爱的孙子,我坚持认为你应该计划在深秋之前来访。让你看看我们的半岛,这份喜悦太明显了,不容放弃。让我用我潦草的字迹,为你描绘那个你尚未见过的家。

从山脚下你沿着一条迂回的小路上行,直到正午,第六个弯处大海便显露出来。我们邻近的林业站坐落在一道山脊上,再往下的低地极易遭受洪水,工程师们不得不疏浚河道并开凿一条平行的运河。我曾在那里当过工程师,后来成了当地政府卑微的客户,也是其养老金的受益人。

这里的人坚韧而充满毅力。他们把孩子教育得很好;理解力和良好的工作取向比财富更受重视。此外,他们保持着一种安静的一致性,尽管没有人逃避诚实的劳动。我不会歪曲他们:有几个爱嫉妒,有几个平庸,还有一个古怪的老守财奴甚至想在一小笔借款的账目上欺骗自己的合伙人。这种渎职行为很明显,他罪行的公布引起了轰动、几乎戏剧性的骚动。坦白说那是一桩冷酷而闷热的丑闻,绝对没有人会赋予他原谅。

不过我不该贬损这里的美。黎明时一个嗓音沙哑的渔夫敲我的窗格,在盘子里卖米和鲜鱼。一条毛虫爬过炉膛;卷曲如餐巾的雾包裹着海湾。我点亮灯,叠起一张租用的地图,感到知足的纤维。我心中孩子气的那一面仍渴望追逐候鸟的迁徙,看它们每个季节重现,聚集在那台旧润滑油泵生锈的坑洞上空。

我承认,我的健康有个缺陷。一种持续的悲怆已落在我胸口,这双侧的疼痛不会兑现任何康复的承诺。这缺陷还不至于严重到要为之悲伤。我拒绝孤独老哲学家的刻板形象;我不是个轻信的傻瓜。当我用航空信或录音带联系老友,或当疗养院的联络官寄来一张不够充分却善意的便条时,仍有一伏电流穿过我身。

别让不同的意见使你分心,也别让你年纪之下的某种市侩骄傲拖延你的决定。有人说这趟旅程迂回;我却说它值得。来吧,我们不会歪曲任何事,只坐在炉边,一直聊到那闷热、弥漫着油脂味的夜晚深处。爱你的祖父。
